safest_plan.entry_basis: Break of the rejected value-area-high (the ~4,600 'flip-high' / white level marked 'Easiest setup' on the 4H chart gold-1879.png) with a body close ABOVE, then a retest back to it — Zero TG 6 Jun scenario 1 (the 'easiest' / safer path). Price on the 5 Jun chart was ~4,419, below this level, so it is NOT yet triggered.

targets.basis: POC / TP1 (white POC line on the 4H chart; Zero live 6 Jun TP 4,800)
VAH / TP2 (green value-area-high band on the 4H chart; Zero live 11 May 'value at high' ~4,823, rounded to the charted band)
Full rotation — chart label 'Full rotation implies $4,960 as technical target' near the 5,000 round number (Zero TG 6 Jun targets 4,800 / 5,000)

safest_plan.stop_basis: An accepted close below the HTF demand zone (~4,350, green band on the 4H chart). Zero stressed price held above demand and 'didn't even close below the EQ' — a close below this band kills the long thesis. A clean sweep of the deeper VAL ~4,303 that RECLAIMS keeps it valid; an accepted close below 4,300 is the harder backstop.

stance_by_source.summary: Demand test held with a strong reaction; 4H reads accumulatory and COT skew still long. Setup valid but untriggered — buy only on a break+retest of the ~4,600 flip-high, or a sweep of ~4,300 that reclaims; otherwise skip. Targets 4,800 / 4,960 / 5,000.
Same two-path long: break value-area-high with body close then retest 4,800/5,000, OR sweep the value-area-low ~4,300, reclaim and resume up. Invalidation: a close below ~4,300.

Graded textbook — already long from the value-area-low flip (~4,650-4,680), aiming for 4,823 then a full rotation to 5,000; wrong on a close below the value-area-low. (Stale anchor: a month older than the June read.)
Patience for a retest of the value-area-low (~4,600-4,620) before longing toward the value-area-high. (Older, levels since superseded.)
Buy the value-area-low rotation zone ~4,600-4,700 targeting 5,000-5,200. (Older; the 5,200 target is stale vs the current structure.)
Earliest read — accumulate 3,600-3,900 on a retest, target 4,200-4,500, weekly close below 3,500 invalidates. Now well below market; superseded by the higher June structure.

conflicts: No directional disagreement — every source is bullish/accumulatory; the contest is about timing, not direction.

The latest read is explicitly UNTRIGGERED and forks two ways: a direct break-up from here, OR a dip to sweep ~4,300 first then reclaim. Because the entry is conditional, do not treat ~4,600 as a live buy until price actually breaks and retests it.

Two different 'VAL' levels appear: the 4H chart labels a previous value-area-low band at ~4,690, while the text's 'VAL ~4,300' is the deeper macro support line (4,303 on the chart). The 4,300 sweep zone is the deep one — don't confuse it with the 4,690 band.
Older April/March levels (DCA 3,600-3,900; targets 5,200 and 4,200-4,500) are stale relative to the June structure now centred ~4,400-4,800; the newer HTF read supersedes them.
plain_english: Every analysis of gold points the same way — up — and the most recent one (5 June) says gold is quietly building a base and held the support that mattered. But it has NOT given a green light yet. You only buy if one of two things happens first: price breaks above roughly 4,600 and then comes back to touch that level and holds, OR it dips to about 4,300, holds there, and recovers. Until one of those triggers, do nothing. If it does trigger, the upside targets are around 4,800, then 4,840, then 4,960-5,000; the idea is wrong if gold closes below about 4,350.
